comments.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This long chain of `or` operators can be hard to read and maintain. For better clarity and to make it easier to modify in the future, consider refactoring this logic into a loop that iterates through the possible sources.

```suggestion
    task_run_id = None
    # The sources are checked in order of preference.
    sources_to_check = [
        memo.get("taskRunId"),
        memo.get("task_run_id"),
        search_attributes.get("mm_task_run_id"),
        params.get("taskRunId"),
        params.get("task_run_id"),
    ]
    for value in sources_to_check:
        if value:
            candidate = str(value).strip()
            if candidate:
                task_run_id = candidate
                break
```

## Code Review

This pull request introduces the tracking and persistence of task_run_id within Temporal execution records. It adds logic to extract this ID from multiple sources during serialization, updates the ExecutionModel schema, and implements a new activity method, _report_task_run_binding, to link Temporal workflow IDs with managed run UUIDs in the database. Additionally, it ensures these IDs are cleared during 'continue as new' operations and includes corresponding unit tests. Feedback suggests refactoring the multi-source ID extraction logic for better readability and using shared constants for the metadata keys to prevent duplication.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

These hardcoded keys (`taskRunId`, `task_run_id`, `mm_task_run_id`) are also used for lookup in `api_service/api/routers/executions.py`. To avoid duplication and reduce the risk of inconsistencies if these keys change in the future, consider defining them as constants in a shared location (e.g., a schemas file) and importing them where needed.
You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

Continue-As-New reruns clear taskRunId from memo/search_attributes, but the execution serializer also reads taskRunId from record.parameters. Since _continue_as_new leaves record.parameters unchanged, a stale taskRunId stored in parameters will still be surfaced after rerun and can keep Live Output bound to an old session. Consider popping taskRunId/task_run_id from record.parameters here as well (and/or stop reading it from parameters if that’s no longer a supported source).
```suggestion
        record.search_attributes = attrs
        params = dict(record.parameters or {})
        params.pop("taskRunId", None)
        params.pop("task_run_id", None)
        record.parameters = params
```

This test sets taskRunId in initial_parameters (stored on record.parameters) and also in memo, but only asserts that memo no longer contains taskRunId after rerun. Given _serialize_execution can also source taskRunId from record.parameters, this test should assert the rerun clears taskRunId from parameters/search_attributes too (or assert the serialized execution no longer includes taskRunId) to prevent regressions.
```suggestion
        assert "taskRunId" not in refreshed.memo
        assert "taskRunId" not in refreshed.parameters
```

Import order here is likely to fail isort/ruff (stdlib imports should come before third-party). `from types import SimpleNamespace` should be grouped with other stdlib imports above `import pytest`.
```suggestion
from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
```

_report_task_run_binding writes the provided run_id directly into execution memo as taskRunId, but unlike _report_live_session it does not validate that run_id is a UUID. Since downstream uses this value to call `/api/task-runs/{id}/live-session` (UUID-keyed), consider parsing/validating run_id (and logging+skipping on invalid input) to avoid persisting unusable bindings.

## Pull request overview Fixes the identifier mismatch between Temporal executions (workflow id) and managed runtime live sessions (task-run UUID) so the task dashboard can reliably resolve and display Live Output for managed runs. **Changes:** - Persist managed `taskRunId` onto the Temporal execution record during managed runtime launch and expose it on execution detail payloads. - Clear stale task-run bindings on Continue-As-New reruns to avoid pointing Live Output at old sessions. - Add/extend unit/regression tests covering execution serialization, managed launch wiring, activity binding, and rerun cleanup. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py` | Adds DB write to bind `workflow_id -> taskRunId` during managed launch activities. | | `moonmind/workflows/temporal/service.py` | Clears task-run binding fields during Continue-As-New reruns. | | `api_service/api/routers/executions.py` | Surfaces `taskRunId` in serialized execution payloads. | | `moonmind/schemas/temporal_models.py` | Adds `task_run_id` / `taskRunId` field to `ExecutionModel`. | | `moonmind/workflows/adapters/managed_agent_adapter.py` | Passes `workflow_id` into managed launch payload so activities can persist the binding. | | `tests/unit/api/routers/test_executions.py` | Adds regression test for `taskRunId` serialization. | | `tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_temporal_service.py` | Extends rerun test to exercise taskRunId cleanup behavior. | | `tests/unit/services/temporal/test_agent_runtime_activities.py` | Adds test asserting binding is reported before launch. | | `tests/unit/workflows/adapters/test_managed_agent_adapter.py` | Asserts `workflow_id` is included in managed launch payload. |
